title: Apply: animal behavior and offspring success
description: Apply your understanding of animal behavior and offspring success in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Apply: animal behavior and offspring success

assessment_items.question_content:
**The following behaviors help birds increase their reproductive success in different ways. Which one most directly increases a bird’s chance of *mating*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A male peacock shows off his large, colorful tail feathers to attract a female.
Downy woodpecker parents bring their offspring food until the offspring are ready to leave the nest.
A bald eagle eats a large amount of prey that it will digest over a period of a few days.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The following behaviors help deer increase their reproductive success in different ways. Which one most directly increases a deer’s chance of *mating*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A deer flashes the white patch of fur under its tail to warn others that a predator is nearby.
A female deer hides her fawn in an area with tall grass while she looks for food.
A male deer uses his antlers to fight for a female.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The following behaviors help birds increase their reproductive success in different ways. Which one most directly increases a bird’s chance of *mating*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A killdeer bird pretends to be injured to distract and lead predators away from its nest.
A male bird-of-paradise dances to attract the attention of a female.
An arctic tern migrates from an area of low resources to an area of high resources.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a herd of adult elephants surrounding a calf. Elephants often carry out this behavior when a predator is near.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely result of this elephant behavior?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A baby elephant surrounded by adult elephants.

choices.content: a higher chance that the adults will have offspring
a higher chance that a predator will attack the calf
a higher chance that the calf will survive to become an adult

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a female lion carefully using her mouth to move her newborn cub to a new den. Lions carry out this behavior to prevent the cub’s scent from building up in one spot.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely result of this lion behavior?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Transporting the cub](https://www.flickr.com/photos/8070463@N03/5208580123)" by Tambako the Jaguar, [CC BY-ND 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nd/2.0/)

options.alt: A female lion carrying a newborn cub in her mouth.

choices.content: a higher chance that the adult will produce offspring
a higher chance that the cub will survive to become an adult
a higher chance that a more comfortable den will be found

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a female orangutan carrying her baby by allowing it to cling to her fur. Female orangutans carry their babies in this way for at least two years.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely result of this orangutan behavior?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A female orangutan with a baby orangutan close to her, clinging to her fur.

choices.content: a higher chance that the baby will survive to become an adult
a higher chance that a predator will attack the baby
a higher chance that the adult will produce offspring

assessment_items.question_content:
In *Chiromantis hansenae* tree frogs, the female parent stays with her egg clutch until the eggs hatch. During this time, the parent cares for the eggs by covering them with her body.

A scientist wanted to study the importance of this behavior in the wild. The scientist found $20$ clutches of frog eggs. Then, the scientist removed the female parent from ten of them. 

The scientist observed what happened to the eggs over time. The results of the study are shown in the following table.

 | Number of clutches that survived | Number of clutches that died from drying out | Number of clutches that were killed by predators
- | :-: | :-: | :-:
Parent present | $8$ | $0$ | $2$
Parent removed | $0$ | $8$ | $2$

**Based on the data in the table, what is the most likely reason that female tree frogs care for their eggs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to prevent the eggs from drying out
to protect the eggs from predators
to decrease the female parent’s reproductive success

assessment_items.question_content:
In a species of ground-dwelling frog, male parents care for their egg clutches until they hatch. 

A scientist wanted to study the importance of this behavior in the wild. The scientist found $20$ clutches of frog eggs. Then, the scientist removed the male parent from ten of them. 

The scientist observed what happened to the eggs over time. The results of the study are shown in the following table.

 | Number of clutches that survived | Number of clutches that were killed by fungus | Number of clutches that were killed by ants
- | :-: | :-: | :-:
Parent present | $9$ | $1$ | $0$
Parent removed | $0$ | $1$ | $9$

**Based on the data in the table, what is the most likely reason that the male frogs care for their eggs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to prevent the eggs from being killed by ants
to protect the eggs from fungus
to decrease the male parent’s reproductive success

assessment_items.question_content:
In *Hylophorbus rufescens* frogs, male parents care for their egg clutches until they hatch. 

A scientist wanted to study the importance of this behavior in the wild. The scientist found $20$ clutches of frog eggs. Then, the scientist removed the male parent from ten of them. 

The scientist observed what happened to the eggs over time. The results of the study are shown in the following table.

 | Number of clutches that survived | Number of clutches that were killed by fungus | Number of clutches that were killed by ants
- | :-: | :-: | :-:
Parent present | $9$ | $1$ | $0$
Parent removed | $0$ | $1$ | $9$

**Based on the data in the table, which statement about parental care in *H. rufescens* frogs is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Parental care helps protect eggs from ants.
Parental care helps protect eggs from fungus.
Parental care helps decrease a parent frog’s reproductive success

assessment_items.question_content:
Snail leeches are a type of predatory worm. After a snail leech lays its eggs, it covers the eggs with its body. After the eggs hatch, the snail leech stays with its young for about a month. During this time, the parent snail leech does not eat.

A scientist claims that these behaviors increase the snail leech’s reproductive success. 

**Which observation best supports the scientist’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Snail leeches reproduce once per year.
Eggs that are covered by a parent’s body are eaten less often by predators.
Snail leeches that cover their eggs lose weight as a result of not eating.

assessment_items.question_content:
After a male midwife toad fertilizes a female’s eggs, he gathers the eggs around his legs. He carries the eggs and keeps them moist for many weeks. When it is time for the eggs to hatch, the toad places them in a calm pool of water.

A scientist claims that these behaviors increase the midwife toad’s reproductive success. 

**Which observation best supports the scientist’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Eggs that are carried by the midwife toad are eaten less often by predators.
Male midwife toads attract the attention of female toads with a whistling sound.
Male midwife toads that carry their eggs use up a large amount of energy.

assessment_items.question_content:
After a male seahorse fertilizes a female’s eggs, he keeps the eggs in his pouch. When the eggs hatch, the offspring stay in the male’s pouch for a few weeks. During this time, the male controls the salt concentration of the water in the pouch. 

A scientist claims that this parental care behavior increases the male seahorse’s reproductive success.

**Which observation best supports the scientist’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Male and female seahorses do a courtship dance before egg fertilization.
Offspring that remain in the male seahorse’s pouch are eaten less often by predators.
Male seahorses use up a large amount of energy controlling the amount of salt in their pouches.
